zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Archibald Gillespic Campbell - Fifth Lord of Lochawe, Knight於1120年之前出生在英國蘇格蘭阿吉爾 安德 布特時，他的父親Sir Cailen Maol Maith Campbell 4th Campbell of Lochow29歲，母親Eiall nic Eth31歲。他和Anna McDonald至少有1個兒子。他去世於1162年在英國蘇格蘭阿吉爾洛霍，埋葬在英國蘇格蘭阿吉爾 安德 布特吉爾可雷南。

nameMeanings.name: Campbell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: nickname from Gaelic cam ‘crooked, bent’ + beul ‘mouth’. As a result of folk etymology, the surname was often represented in Latin documents as de bello campo ‘of the fair field’, which led to the name sometimes being ‘translated’ into Anglo-Norman French as Beauchamp .
Irish (North Armagh): adopted for Gaelic Mac Cathmhaoil ‘son of Cathmhaol’ (literally ‘battle chief’): see Caulfield and Cowell .
English: variant of   Camel  , under the influence of the Scottish name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Campell
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire) and English: variant of   Campbell   (see also   Camel  ).

variants.name: Beauchamp
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Antoine, Jacques, Yves, Andre, Armand, Emile, Fernand, Marcel, Marthe, Remi, Alcide, Camille.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of several places in France, for example in Manche and Somme, that are named with Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + champ(s) ‘field, plain’ (from Latin bello campo ‘beautiful field’). In English the surname is generally pronounced Beecham . Compare Bushaw .
English: in some cases, an Anglo-Norman French adaptation of Scottish Campbell , by translation into French of the folk etymology de campo bello ‘from the fair field’.

variants.name: Camel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Napoleon, Patrice.

English (of Norman origin) and French: nickname from Norman French came(i)l ‘camel’ (from Latin camelus, classical Greek kamēlos), possibly denoting a clumsy person or someone with long legs. It may also be a topographic or habitational name referring to a house with a sign depicting a camel (although surnames derived from house and inn signs are rare in English); compare 5 below.
English: possibly a habitational name from Queen Camel and West Camel in Somerset, early recorded as Camel(le), possibly a Celtic name from canto- ‘border, district’ and mēl ‘bare hill’.

nameMeanings.name: Archibald
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of Norman French origin, from a Germanic (Frankish) personal name derived from ercan ‘genuine’ + bald ‘bold, brave’. It has long been associated with Scotland, where it is in regular use as the English equivalent of Gaelic Gilleasbaig ( see Gillespie ).

variants.name: Archibold
meanings: Of Norman French origin, from a Germanic (Frankish) personal name derived from ercan ‘genuine’ + bald ‘bold, brave’. It has long been associated with Scotland, where it is in regular use as the English equivalent of Gaelic Gilleasbaig ( see Gillespie ).

nameMeanings.name: Gillespic
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Anglicized form of Gaelic Gille Easbaig ‘bishop's servant’. Gille Easbaig Cambeul was the founder of Clan Campbell.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Archibald Gillespic Campbell - Fifth Lord of Lochawe, Knight was born before 1120, in Argyll and Bute, Scotland, United Kingdom, his father, Sir Cailen Maol Maith Campbell 4th Campbell of Lochow, was 29 and his mother, Eiall nic Eth, was 31. He had at least 1 son with Anna McDonald. He died in 1162, in Lochawe, Argyll, Scotland, United Kingdom, and was buried in Kilchrenan, Argyll and Bute, Scotland, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Archibald Gillespic Campbell - Fifth Lord of Lochawe, Knight est né avant 1120, à Argyll and Bute, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir Cailen Maol Maith Campbell 4th Campbell of Lochow, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Eiall nic Eth, avait 31 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Anna McDonald. Il est décédé en 1162, à Lochawe, Argyll, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, et a été enterré à Kilchrenan, Argyll and Bute, Écosse, Royaume-Uni.

Er hatte mit Anna McDonald mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1162 in Lochawe, Argyll, Schottland, United Kingdom und wurde in Kilchrenan, Argyll and Bute, Schottland, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Archibald Gillespic Campbell - Fifth Lord of Lochawe, Knight è nato il prima del 1120 nel Argyll and Bute, Scozia, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Sir Cailen Maol Maith Campbell 4th Campbell of Lochow, aveva 29 anni e il suo madre, Eiall nic Eth, aveva 31 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Anna McDonald. Morì nel 1162, nel Lochawe, Argyll, Scozia, Regno Unito, e fu sepolto a Kilchrenan, Argyll and Bute, Scozia, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Archibald Gillespic Campbell - Fifth Lord of Lochawe, Knightが1120年以前にイギリススコットランドアルグイル アンド ブトで生まれた時，父Sir Cailen Maol Maith Campbell 4th Campbell of Lochowは29歳，母Eiall nic Ethは31歳であった。彼はAnna McDonaldとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は1162年にイギリススコットランドアルグイルロチャウで死去し，イギリススコットランドアルグイル アンド ブトキルチレナンにて埋葬された。

ko.Kilchrenan, Argyll and Bute, Scotland: 영국 스코틀랜드 아그이 앤드 부트 킬크리난
ko.lifeSummary: Sir Archibald Gillespic Campbell - Fifth Lord of Lochawe, Knight(이)가 1120년 이전 영국 스코틀랜드 아그이 앤드 부트에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Cailen Maol Maith Campbell 4th Campbell of Lochow(은)는 29세였고, 그의 어머니 Eiall nic Eth(은)는 31세였다. 그는 Anna McDonald(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1162년 영국 스코틀랜드 아그이 럭아베에서 사망했으며 영국 스코틀랜드 아그이 앤드 부트 킬크리난에 묻혔다.

ru.Kilchrenan, Argyll and Bute, Scotland: килчренан, аргилл анд бут, Шотландия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Archibald Gillespic Campbell - Fifth Lord of Lochawe, Knight родился перед 1120 г. в местности аргилл анд бут, Шотландия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Sir Cailen Maol Maith Campbell 4th Campbell of Lochow было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Eiall nic Eth, 31. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Anna McDonald. Он умер в 1162 г. в местности лочав, аргилл, Шотландия, Великобритания и был похоронен в местности килчренан, аргилл анд бут, Шотландия, Великобритания.
